name: Trilobite
imagePrompt: Olenoides trilobite, segmented exoskeleton with three lobes running head-to-tail, calcified dorsal shield, paired walking legs and gills underneath, compound eyes on the head shield, mottled brown-grey carapace with subtle iridescence, 8cm length, scientifically accurate paleontological reconstruction
description: A grazer and scavenger of the seafloor. Walks on dozens of paired legs in a slow rhythmic gait; can roll into a defensive ball when threatened. Senses the world through compound eyes and chemosensory antennae. Lives in groups, often half-buried in sediment.
